programme.title: PL STORIES EP10: IPSWICH TOWN
programme.sub-title: In the first senior manager’s job of his career, Kieran McKenna has achieved something nobody else has managed this decade: Winning back-to-back promotions from League One to the Premier League, in the process ending Ipswich’s 22-year absence from top flight football. We go behind the scenes with the team as they prepare for the opening game of the new season whilst also exploring the club’s unique history.
programme.desc: In the first senior manager’s job of his career, Kieran McKenna has achieved something nobody else has managed this decade: Winning back-to-back promotions from League One to the Premier League, in the process ending Ipswich’s 22-year absence from top flight football. programme.title: PL STORIES EP28: TEENAGE TRAILBLAZERS
programme.sub-title: We explore the remarkable journeys of players who made their mark on the Premier League stage as teenagers. From prodigious talents to record-breakers, we tell the stories of these fearless young stars who defied expectations and changed the game forever.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP30: (Subbed) BASQUE MANAGERS
programme.sub-title: We explore the remarkable impact of the Basque region on the Premier League, focusing on the careers of the four current Basque managers who are leaving their mark in England’s topflight. From tactical brilliance to relentless passion, this program delves into why the Basque Country has produced such a high number of successful managers and how their unique style has left a lasting legacy in the Premier League.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP20: COLE PALMER
programme.sub-title: This week’s Big Interview sees two of Chelsea’s biggest stars from different generations come together for a football masterclass. Considered one of the most technically gifted players the Premier League has ever seen, Gianfranco Zola visits the Chelsea training ground to talk to the man currently lighting up the Premier League, Cole Palmer, about various aspects of his game. From free kicks to finishing, the pair watch and talk through clips of their play and then recreate those moments on the pitch. This one is not to be missed.
programme.desc: This week’s Big Interview sees two of Chelsea’s biggest stars from different generations come together for a football masterclass. From free kicks to finishing, the pair watch and talk through clips of their play and then recreate those moments on the pitch. This one is not to be missed.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP32: (Subbed) THOMAS TUCHEL
programme.sub-title: As Thomas Tuchel begins his journey as England’s new manager, PL Stories dives into his journey to the top, tracing his rise from playing career to football management. After facing financial struggles and working in a bar, Tuchel’s determination led him to become one of the Premier League's most respected managers. We showcase his unique path, blending hard work, passion, and tactical innovation to reach the pinnacle of football.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP11: JARRED GILLETT - THROUGH THE EYES OF A REFEREE
programme.sub-title: Jarred Gillett made history earlier this year when he became the first Premier League referee ever to be fitted with a special head-mounted camera during Crystal Palace’s home victory over Manchester United. PL Stories is granted exclusive access to Gillet and his officiating team on matchday, offering fans a unique insight into refereeing at the highest level. Premier League match officials and in particular referees are often the most scrutinised individuals in the game and this show reveals how difficult a task they have.

programme.sub-title: Newcastle United through and through, Dan Burn epitomises what it means to pull on the famous black and white striped shirt. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP22: HOWARD WEBB
programme.sub-title: Head of the PGMOL, Howard Webb is in the studio for this week’s Big Interview, candidly opening up about his personal story and how he got into the game. Webb reflects on various moments throughout his life and career including that infamous decision not to issue a red card in the 2010 World Cup Final, implementing VAR and where he sees the technology going, as well as his hopes that referees will continue to be more inclusive in the Premier League.

programme.title: GENERATION xG MON 06 JAN
programme.sub-title: A deep dive into the tactical trends and statistical insights of modern football with input from players, managers and data experts from the 'fanalyst' community. This programme will analyse the on-field action in forensic detail and give hard core Premier League fans a new insight into what is happening on the pitch and why.

programme.title: REVIEW OF THE SEASON 2023/2024 (MNC)
programme.sub-title: The 2023/24 Premier League season will be forever remembered as one of the greatest campaigns ever. From an absorbing title race, Jürgen Klopp leaving Liverpool to Luton living the dream in the top-flight – Review of the Season documents all of the thrilling stories to emerge across the 380 matches.
programme.desc: The 2023/24 Premier League season will be forever remembered as one of the greatest campaigns ever. programme.title: PL REWIND EP04: BOU v LUT 23/24
programme.sub-title: In December 2023, Bournemouth v Luton is abandoned when Tom Lockyer faced a life-threatening situation on the pitch. He survived and watched the rematch, which turned into a Premier League classic. Andoni Iraola, Lewis Cook and Antoine Semenyo tell the story.
programme.desc: In December 2023, Bournemouth v Luton is abandoned when Tom Lockyer faced a life-threatening situation on the pitch. He survived and watched the rematch, which turned into a Premier League classic. Andoni Iraola, Lewis Cook and Antoine Semenyo tell the story.

programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP22: HOWARD WEBB
programme.sub-title: Head of the PGMOL, Howard Webb is in the studio for this week’s Big Interview, candidly opening up about his personal story and how he got into the game. Webb reflects on various moments throughout his life and career including that infamous decision not to issue a red card in the 2010 World Cup Final, implementing VAR and where he sees the technology going, as well as his hopes that referees will continue to be more inclusive in the Premier League.

programme.sub-title: As Thomas Tuchel begins his journey as England’s new manager, PL Stories dives into his journey to the top, tracing his rise from playing career to football management. After facing financial struggles and working in a bar, Tuchel’s determination led him to become one of the Premier League's most respected managers. We showcase his unique path, blending hard work, passion, and tactical innovation to reach the pinnacle of football.

programme.title: PL REWIND EP04: BOU v LUT 23/24
programme.sub-title: In December 2023, Bournemouth v Luton is abandoned when Tom Lockyer faced a life-threatening situation on the pitch. He survived and watched the rematch, which turned into a Premier League classic. Andoni Iraola, Lewis Cook and Antoine Semenyo tell the story.
programme.desc: In December 2023, Bournemouth v Luton is abandoned when Tom Lockyer faced a life-threatening situation on the pitch. He survived and watched the rematch, which turned into a Premier League classic. Andoni Iraola, Lewis Cook and Antoine Semenyo tell the story.

programme.title: PL MOMENTS EP09: THIERRY HENRY'S TOP 10 PL MOMENTS
programme.sub-title: Arguably the greatest striker the Premier League has ever seen, Thierry Henry sits down with Will Perry to reveal his Top 10 Premier League Moments.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP39: KICKS AND CULTURE STATESIDE
programme.sub-title: The Premier League is seeing a big boost in popularity amongst the Black American population and we travelled Stateside to learn more about this growing appeal. The show is led by Aaron Dolores, football fan and founder of Black Arrow, a creative movement on a quest to promote the intersection of football and black culture, not just in the US but worldwide. He takes us on a journey where we meet varying characters from the Mayor of Birmingham Alabama, Randall Woodfin, former players Tim Howard and Jozy Altidore, to current player Chris Richards as well as authentic Black American fans immersed in Premier League culture.
programme.desc: The Premier League is seeing a big boost in popularity amongst the Black American population and we travelled Stateside to learn more about this growing appeal. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP20: COLE PALMER
programme.sub-title: This week’s Big Interview sees two of Chelsea’s biggest stars from different generations come together for a football masterclass. Considered one of the most technically gifted players the Premier League has ever seen, Gianfranco Zola visits the Chelsea training ground to talk to the man currently lighting up the Premier League, Cole Palmer, about various aspects of his game. From free kicks to finishing, the pair watch and talk through clips of their play and then recreate those moments on the pitch. This one is not to be missed.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP10: IPSWICH TOWN
programme.sub-title: In the first senior manager’s job of his career, Kieran McKenna has achieved something nobody else has managed this decade: Winning back-to-back promotions from League One to the Premier League, in the process ending Ipswich’s 22-year absence from top flight football. We go behind the scenes with the team as they prepare for the opening game of the new season whilst also exploring the club’s unique history.
programme.desc: In the first senior manager’s job of his career, Kieran McKenna has achieved something nobody else has managed this decade: Winning back-to-back promotions from League One to the Premier League, in the process ending Ipswich’s 22-year absence from top flight football. programme.title: THE BIG INTERVIEW EP19: BASSEY x IWOBI
programme.sub-title: Fulham’s Calvin Bassey and Alex Iwobi have been instrumental in the club’s resurgence. Off the field, they’ve shared similar experiences playing for Nigeria, their charity work in the community, as well as gaining an online following for their comical social media clips. In this week’s Big Interview, the pair sit down to talk in further depth about their friendship, their backstories, the importance of their Nigerian heritage and how Marco Silva has helped turn Fulham into one of the Premier League’s toughest opponents.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP10: IPSWICH TOWN
programme.sub-title: In the first senior manager’s job of his career, Kieran McKenna has achieved something nobody else has managed this decade: Winning back-to-back promotions from League One to the Premier League, in the process ending Ipswich’s 22-year absence from top flight football. We go behind the scenes with the team as they prepare for the opening game of the new season whilst also exploring the club’s unique history.
programme.desc: In the first senior manager’s job of his career, Kieran McKenna has achieved something nobody else has managed this decade: Winning back-to-back promotions from League One to the Premier League, in the process ending Ipswich’s 22-year absence from top flight football. We go behind the scenes with the team as they prepare for the opening game of the new season whilst also exploring the club’s unique history.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP06: THE ENTERTAINERS
programme.sub-title: Nicknamed ‘The Entertainers’, Kevin Keegan’s Newcastle team of the 1995-96 season set the Premier League alight with their confidence and flair that took them agonisingly close to winning their first Premier League title. In this show, we speak to those that were part of that incredible journey and reflect on some of those unforgettable moments that captured hearts and forever changing the club's legacy.
programme.desc: Nicknamed ‘The Entertainers’, Kevin Keegan’s Newcastle team of the 1995-96 season set the Premier League alight with their confidence and flair that took them agonisingly close to winning their first Premier League title. In this show, we speak to those that were part of that incredible journey and reflect on some of those unforgettable moments that captured hearts and forever changing the club's legacy.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP31: (Subbed) NOLBERTO SOLANO
programme.sub-title: We reflect on the life and career of Nolberto Solano, one of South America's football legends. From his early days in Peru to his pivotal role in the Premier League, we explore his journey, triumphs, and challenges. Featuring exclusive interviews and rare footage, we capture the essence of a player who transcended borders and inspired generations.
programme.desc: We reflect on the life and career of Nolberto Solano, one of South America's football legends. From his early days in Peru to his pivotal role in the Premier League, we explore his journey, triumphs, and challenges. Featuring exclusive interviews and rare footage, we capture the essence of a player who transcended borders and inspired generations.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP25: (Subbed) HERNAN CRESPO
programme.sub-title: We delve into the remarkable journey of Argentine football legend Hernán Crespo. From his early days as a striker with clubs like Parma, Lazio, and Chelsea, to his evolution into a title winning coach, Crespo's career has been filled with highs and challenges. We follow his transition from player to manager and join him at Al Ain, where we get a look into how he navigates the pressures and ambitions of managing one of the UAE's top football clubs.
programme.desc: We delve into the remarkable journey of Argentine football legend Hernán Crespo. From his early days as a striker with clubs like Parma, Lazio, and Chelsea, to his evolution into a title winning coach, Crespo's career has been filled with highs and challenges. We follow his transition from player to manager and join him at Al Ain, where we get a look into how he navigates the pressures and ambitions of managing one of the UAE's top football clubs.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP26: THE GREATEST GAME EVER PLAYED
programme.sub-title: We gather a panel of experts to tackle a nearly impossible question: What truly is the Greatest Premier League Game Ever? Join two ex-footballers, a seasoned commentator, and a top football journalist as they lock horns in a passionate roundtable discussion. The panel revisits iconic moments, dissecting tactics, and legendary performances. Expect banter, bold claims, and maybe even a few surprises, as they strive to settle the debate—or perhaps keep it alive. Whether reliving “Fergie Time” magic, Leicester’s miracle season, or Aguero’s legendary finish, this episode celebrates the unfiltered drama that only Premier League history can offer.
programme.desc: We gather a panel of experts to tackle a nearly impossible question: What truly is the Greatest Premier League Game Ever? programme.title: PL STORIES EP32: (Subbed) THOMAS TUCHEL
programme.sub-title: As Thomas Tuchel begins his journey as England’s new manager, PL Stories dives into his journey to the top, tracing his rise from playing career to football management. After facing financial struggles and working in a bar, Tuchel’s determination led him to become one of the Premier League's most respected managers. We showcase his unique path, blending hard work, passion, and tactical innovation to reach the pinnacle of football.

programme.title: PL STORIES EP29: ANDY ROBERTSON
programme.sub-title: From his humble beginnings at Queen’s Park in Scotland to becoming a key player for Liverpool, Andy Robertson’s rise is a tale of resilience, hard work, and determination. We delve in to his early struggles, his breakthrough at Hull City, and his crucial role in Liverpool's historic Premier League and Champions League triumphs. Featuring exclusive interviews with teammates, coaches, and Robertson himself, this episode celebrates his leadership, passion, and relentless spirit on and off the pitch.
